reasoning: As of October 26, 2025, there are no reported human cases or outbreaks associated with the bat coronaviruses discovered earlier in the year (such as HKU5-CoV-2 or the Brazilian strain). The WHO has not declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) for any new coronavirus. For a novel pathogen to jump to humans, spread globally, and cross the threshold for a formal WHO pandemic declaration within the two remaining months of the year is extraordinarily unlikely. The timeline rules out this possibility almost completely, bringing the probability down to the minimum allowed bound.
